reference.description: Single flat map of ALL old→new string replacements across all passes. Sorted longest-first. Use this for mass replacement tools (Java, sed). Contains ~1400 unique entries covering artists, song titles, game/anime refs.
reference.usage: Primary input for MassReplacer.java and apply-fictional-names.sh

reference.description: Pass 1 data: PascalCase artist IDs → Fictional-{Adj}{Noun} mappings, plus all textual variants (lowercase, titled, hyphenated). Generated deterministically via MD5 hash from scripts/apply_fictional_names.py.

sections.section: All text variants → same Fictional name
PascalCase ID → list of variant strings

reference.description: Pass 2 data: keyword-level replacements for song titles, artist names missed by pass 1, and game/anime franchise references. Uses Fictional-Kw-{8hex} naming via MD5. ~1947 rules with case variants.

Pass 3 data: remaining multi-word artist names, classical composers, concatenated/hyphenated forms for dict keys. ~270 regex rules. Has ambiguous-word context restrictions (word-boundary + lookbehind).
JoJo's Bizarre Adventure character → song mapping. ~120 entries. Used by apply_jozep_refs.py for file-copy operations. Already post-fictionalised (artist names in values are Fictional-*).

script.description: First pass: 163 PascalCase artist IDs → Fictional-{Adj}{Noun}. Track filenames → Fictional-Track-{8hex}.mp3. Deterministic via MD5 hash. Handles JSON key replacement.
Second pass: ~1947 keyword-level rules for song titles, game/anime refs, remaining artist names. Case-variant expansion (Title, UPPER, hyphenated).
Third pass: ~270 regex rules for multi-word artist names, classical composers, concat forms. Ambiguous words get context-restricted patterns.
JoJo reference handler: scans Artist/ subfolders, matches file stems against jozep-refs.yml entries, copies matches to Artist/Fictional-JozepRef/.
Copies already-renamed Fictional-VidaSimu tracks to correct Genre/Mood folders. Hardcoded filename sets (26 files).

tool.description: High-performance string replacer. Reads the merged JSON map and applies all replacements to every text file under a target directory. Longest-match-first ordering. Single pass per file.
Full pipeline orchestrator: cleanup → Java mass replace → Python passes → verification. Supports --apply, --java-only, --py-only modes.
Pre-ingestion cleanup: delete images in media/prints dirs, delete old .html in docs/maps/ (keeps most recent date folder).

convention.generator: MD5 hash of PascalCase artist ID → _ADJ[h%50] + _NOUN[(h>>8)%72]
MD5 hash of mp3 stem → first 8 hex chars. Preserves _part_NNN suffixes.
MD5 hash of lowercase keyword → first 8 hex chars

convention.description: JoJo standalone → Fictional-Jozep. JoJo's Bizarre Adventure → Fictional-JozepJourneys

protected-strings.category: Rock, Metal, Pop, Jazz, Blues, Classical, Electronic, HipHop, R&B, Country, Folk, Punk, Reggae, Soul, Funk, Grunge, NuMetal, Metalcore, PostRock, Shoegaze, AlternativeRock, AlternativeMetal, ProgressiveRock, ProgressiveMetal, SymphonicMetal, IndustrialMetal, DeathMetal, BlackMetal, ThrashMetal, PowerMetal, DoomMetal, StoneRock, PostPunk, GothicRock, DreamPop, Ambient, DarkAmbient, Chillwave, Synthwave, WorldMusic, LatinPop, BossaNova, Samba, MPB, Sertanejo, Forró, Pagode, AxéMusic, AnimeOST, GameOST, FilmOST, Soundtrack

All mood labels from docs/guidelines/moods.txt (Aggressive, Chill, Dark, Dreamy, Energetic, Ethereal, Euphoric, Happy, Haunting, Hopeful, Intense, Melancholic, Nostalgic, Peaceful, Powerful, Rebellious, Reflective, Romantic, Sad, Serene, Triumphant, Upbeat, etc.)
Artist, Genre, Mood, singles, albums, classified, Various, Unclassified

workflow.step: Copy/merge new source files into the workspace
